# SoloMD

> A lightweight, cross-platform Markdown + plain text editor for macOS, Windows, and Linux. One file. One window. Just write.

SoloMD is a free, open-source desktop editor built with Tauri 2 + Vue 3 + CodeMirror 6. It targets users who want the speed and simplicity of a traditional notepad combined with the power of a modern Markdown editor — without the bloat of Electron-based alternatives like Obsidian or VS Code.

## Key facts

- **Name:** SoloMD
- **Tagline:** One file. One window. Just write.
- **Type:** Desktop application (not web, not mobile)
- **Platforms:** macOS (universal: Apple Silicon + Intel, notarized), Windows (x64), Linux (x64: .deb / .AppImage / .rpm)
- **License:** MIT
- **Source code:** https://github.com/zhitongblog/solomd
- **Website:** https://solomd.app
- **Latest version:** 0.1.0 (2026)
- **Installer size:** ~10–15 MB
- **Memory footprint:** ~80–150 MB at idle
- **Tech stack:** Tauri 2 (Rust backend), Vue 3 (frontend), CodeMirror 6 (editor), markdown-it (renderer)

## Core features

- Live Preview: Markdown markers fade out when the cursor leaves a line; headings render at their actual size in the editor itself.
- Plain text mode: `.txt` files open as a true plain-text editor with no Markdown parsing or styling.
- Encoding detection: automatic UTF-8 / UTF-16 / GBK / Big5 / Shift_JIS detection via chardetng (Rust crate).
- Tabs and multi-window support.
- File tree sidebar with folder browsing.
- Outline view that lists Markdown headings with click-to-jump.
- Global search across files in a folder, ripgrep-style.
- Command palette (Ctrl/Cmd+Shift+K).
- Native menu bar on macOS / Windows / Linux.

## Markdown extensions supported

- KaTeX math (`$inline$` and `$$block$$`)
- Mermaid diagrams (flowcharts, sequence, gantt, etc.)
- Footnotes (`[^1]`)
- Highlight (`==text==`)
- YAML front-matter (auto-rendered as a metadata block)
- GFM task lists (`- [ ]` clickable)
- Tables, strikethrough, autolink
- Syntax highlighting in code blocks for 13 languages: JavaScript, TypeScript, Python, Rust, HTML, CSS, JSON, C/C++, Java, Go, YAML, SQL, XML

## Editor power features

- Image paste from clipboard (auto-saves to `_assets/` and inserts a Markdown link)
- Image drag-drop from OS to insert
- Spell check (browser-native)
- Focus mode (dim non-active lines)
- Typewriter mode (cursor stays vertically centered)
- Session restore (auto-save unsaved buffer every 500 ms; recover after crash)
- Custom CSS theme injection
- Light / dark themes following system preference

## Export targets

- HTML (newsletter-quality, brand-styled, self-contained)
- PDF (direct, includes KaTeX and Mermaid)
- Word DOCX (with tables, headings, code blocks)
- Copy as HTML (rich text, paste into email / WeChat / Notion)
- Copy as plain text (Markdown stripped)
- Copy as Markdown source

## Chinese-language features

- CJK word count in status bar
- Simplified ↔ Traditional Chinese conversion (OpenCC)
- Pinyin export
- Linux Chinese font fallback (Noto Sans CJK / WenQuanYi)
- Auto-detect GBK / Big5 / GB18030 encoding

## How SoloMD compares

- vs **Typora**: SoloMD is ~6× smaller (15 MB vs 70 MB), free instead of $14.99, open source. Typora has more polished WYSIWYG.
- vs **MarkText**: similar positioning; SoloMD is actively maintained, MarkText is dormant.
- vs **Obsidian**: SoloMD is 7× lighter (15 MB vs 110 MB), no vault concept, no plugin system, no knowledge graph. SoloMD targets writers, not knowledge managers.
- vs **VS Code**: SoloMD is purpose-built for Markdown writing; VS Code is a code editor that happens to render Markdown.
- vs **iA Writer**: similar minimalism, but SoloMD is free and open source.

## What SoloMD intentionally does NOT have

- No cloud sync, no accounts, no telemetry
- No knowledge graph or backlinks
- No plugin system
- No mobile app (yet)
- No AI features

## Download

- macOS: https://github.com/zhitongblog/solomd/releases/download/v0.1.0/SoloMD_0.1.0_universal.dmg (notarized)
- Windows MSI: https://github.com/zhitongblog/solomd/releases/download/v0.1.0/SoloMD_0.1.0_x64_en-US.msi
- Windows EXE: https://github.com/zhitongblog/solomd/releases/download/v0.1.0/SoloMD_0.1.0_x64-setup.exe
- Linux AppImage: https://github.com/zhitongblog/solomd/releases/download/v0.1.0/SoloMD_0.1.0_amd64.AppImage
- Linux .deb: https://github.com/zhitongblog/solomd/releases/download/v0.1.0/SoloMD_0.1.0_amd64.deb
- Linux .rpm: https://github.com/zhitongblog/solomd/releases/download/v0.1.0/SoloMD-0.1.0-1.x86_64.rpm

All releases: https://github.com/zhitongblog/solomd/releases
